overview: Processes WCML/ICML (InCopy Markup Language) documents, which are XML-based files used by Adobe InDesign/InCopy. This filter is very similar to the [IDML Filter](IDML_Filter) and handles `.wcml` and `.icml` file extensions.

extraction.extractNotes.help: Extracts the content of `<Note>` elements in the ICML document as translator notes, making them visible to translators as contextual information.

extraction.extractMasterSpreads.help:
Extracts translatable content from master spreads if they exist in the document. When disabled, only normal spreads are extracted.

Master spreads typically contain repeating elements like headers, footers, and page numbers that appear across multiple pages.

extraction.simplifyCodes.help: Reduces the number of inline codes by re-grouping adjacent codes when possible. This produces cleaner segments for translators by merging consecutive formatting tags into single inline codes.

extraction.skipThreshold.help:
Maximum size for spread files in **KBytes**. Any spread file above this value will either generate an error or be skipped from extraction.

This is useful for skipping large spread files that may contain only graphics and would require too much memory to process.
extraction.skipThreshold.values: Size in KBytes (1–32000). Default is 1000 KB.
notes: Skipped files are **not** checked for translatable text — content in oversized spreads will be silently excluded from extraction.

extraction.newTuOnBr.help:
Controls whether `
` (hard return) elements cause a paragraph break, starting a new text unit. By default, hard returns are kept as inline codes within the same text unit. Enable this when translators need to handle text segments separated by hard returns independently.

processingNotes: The ICML filter is very similar to the [IDML Filter](IDML_Filter) and shares much of the same processing logic.
ICML (InCopy Markup Language) is an XML-based format used by Adobe InDesign and InCopy.
